q: A parallel-plate capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
Charging a capacitor means connecting the capacitor to a resistor.
The surface charge density on each plate is $\sigma = \frac{Q}{A}$.
If the plates are very close together, the electric field between the plates is non-uniform.
The value of the electric field between the plates can be expressed in terms of surface charge density $\sigma$ as $E = \frac{\sigma}{\epsilon}$.
The magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals $E d$.
The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is directly proportional to the separation of the plates.
For a given insulating material the capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to the area of its plates.
The permittivity of vacuum $\epsilon_0$ is $8.85 \times 10^{-12}\text{ C}^2 \text{ N}^{-1} \text{ m}^{-2}$.
For a vacuum, the dielectric constant $\kappa$ is equal to zero.
The dielectric constant of water at $18^\circ\text{C}$ is $81$.
The dielectric constant of an insulating material is the ratio of its permittivity to that of a vacuum.
If there is a vacuum between its plates, its capacitance $C_0$ is $C_0 = \frac{\epsilon_0 d}{A}$.
The ratio of the capacitance of a capacitor with an insulating material to that with a vacuum is called the dielectric constant.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected from a battery and the air medium between its plates is replaced by an insulating material, the potential difference is found to increase.
When an insulating material is inserted into a disconnected charged capacitor, the charge $Q$ of the capacitor does not change.
Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ less than unity.
A dielectric provides an increase in capacitance.
A dielectric provides possible mechanical support between the plates, which allows the plates to be close together without electrical contact.
Film capacitors range from $10\%$ to $0.01\%$ in tolerance.
Electrolytic capacitors are used for energy storage in flash devices.
Smaller units of supercapacitors are used as power backup for static random-access memory (SRAM).
Capacitors can also be used in a time-dependent circuit (timer circuit).
The relationship between charge, capacitance, and potential difference can be written as $Q = CV$.
The ratio of potential differences $\frac{V_0}{V}$ is equal to the dielectric constant $\kappa$.
A dielectric increases the maximum operating voltage of a capacitor.
The physical size of a capacitor changes when a dielectric is inserted.
The dielectric may be able to withstand a larger electric field between the plates than air.
The permittivity of free space $\varepsilon_0$ is equal to $8.85 \times 10^{-12}\text{ C}^2\text{ N}^{-1}\text{ m}^{-2}$.
When the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is doubled, its capacitance increases.
When the distance between two parallel plates having charges of equal magnitude and opposite signs is reduced, the capacitance decreases.
When an insulating material is inserted between the conductors of a capacitor, its capacitance increases.
